dependencies: TSMC for production of wafers for HPC, FPGA and Adaptive SoC products at 7nm or smaller nodes
GLOBALFOUNDRIES for wafer purchases at 12nm and 14nm technology nodes
Third-party wafer foundries for all silicon wafer fabrication
Third-party ATMP partners in Asia-Pacific region for assembly, test, mark and packaging
ATMP joint ventures with Tongfu Microelectronics provide majority of ATMP services
Limited number of suppliers for certain equipment, materials and substrates
Single or limited suppliers for certain IC packages, memory, PCBs, interposers, substrates and capacitors
risks: Dependence on third-party manufacturers for all product manufacturing
No long-term commitment contracts with some third-party manufacturing suppliers
Manufacturers could prioritize capacity for other customers or competitors
Supply constraints from third-party manufacturing suppliers
Limited control over delivery schedules, yield, cycle times, quality assurance
Risk of misappropriation of intellectual property by manufacturers
Concentration risk - TSMC manufactures all products at 7nm or smaller nodes
ATMP JVs provide majority of services with no guarantee of fulfilling long-term requirements
Essential equipment, materials or substrates may not be available
Certain materials available only from limited or sole suppliers
Supply chain disruptions from natural disasters, power outages, water shortages, heat events
Geopolitical tensions between China and Taiwan could disrupt Taiwan-based operations
Climate change impacts on supplier operations and water/energy availability

regulatory_risks: Export control regulations restricting sale of AI technologies to embargoed countries
U.S. export controls on advanced computing items to China (ECCN 3A090, 4A090)
Entity List restrictions - THATIC and THATIC JV added to Entity List in June 2019
Notified Advanced Computing (NAC) notification requirements for certain products to China
International Traffic and Arms Regulations (ITAR) administered by U.S. Department of State
Export Administration Regulation (EAR) governing export and re-export of products
Potential future tariffs, trade protection measures, import/export regulations
Deemed export restrictions affecting R&D teams outside United States
Environmental laws and regulations on materials used in products and manufacturing
EU and China restrictions on use of lead and other materials in electronic products
ENERGY STAR specifications and energy consumption limits in EU, Australia, California, China
Conflict minerals reporting requirements (SEC, EU, China)
Forced labor regulations in supply chains
General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) in EU and UK
California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) and California Privacy Rights Act (CPRA)
EU AI Act adopted in 2024 with phased implementation
Evolving AI-related regulations and intellectual property laws
Climate-related disclosure requirements and reporting obligations
Pillar Two global minimum tax (15%) implemented in many countries
CHIPS Act of 2022 providing financial incentives to U.S. semiconductor industry
Superfund Act environmental liability for contaminated sites
California Regional Water Quality Control Board clean-up requirements

sanction_risks: U.S. sanctions and export control revisions against Russia, Belarus, DNR and LNR regions of Ukraine due to Ukraine conflict
Entity List designation of Chinese entities including THATIC and THATIC JV
U.S.-China trade tensions and ongoing additions of Chinese companies to Entity List
Restrictions on advanced computing and semiconductor manufacturing technologies to China
Potential prohibition of shipments to China or customers with ultimate parent in D5 countries
Risk of retaliatory actions from foreign governments
Geopolitical tensions between China and Taiwan could disrupt operations of Taiwan-based foundries and manufacturers
Ukraine-Russia and Israel-Hamas conflicts could escalate with negative impacts on global economy
U.S. involvement in armed conflicts impacting sales and supply chain
